exemplar.title: To what extent do violent video games influence aggressive behaviour in adolescents?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent do violent video games influence aggressive behaviour in adolescents?

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: The topic is communicated accurately and consistently, with precise definitions and clear signposting throughout.
The research question is sharply formulated and remains central to each section.
Methodology is appropriate and varied, with clear evidence of informed source selection and a coherent plan reflected in the table of contents.
weaknesses: Minor inconsistency in the overview (stating âreview 0 studiesâ despite actual coverage) slightly detracts from precision.
Occasional lapses in linking methodological choices directly back to the research question.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work fully meets the top-band descriptor: topic, purpose and focus are clear and sustained; the RQ is focused and well connected to the discussion; methodology is comprehensive and justified. Only very minor inconsistencies prevent any deduction from the uppermost mark, supporting a full score of 6.

strengths: Terminology and concepts are used accurately and consistently, with appropriate context and explanation.
Subject knowledge is strong, and sources are applied coherently to support claims.
weaknesses: Occasional notational inaccuracy (confusing r and rÂ²) disrupts statistical precision.
A minor citation error (misspelt author name) slightly affects source credibility.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates excellent understanding and coherent application of sources and terminology, consistent with the 5â6 band. However, the few notational slips and citation inaccuracies justify awarding 5 rather than the maximum.

strengths: Research selection is broad, balanced and directly relevant to the research question.
Analysis is clear with mostly well-supported conclusions using the GRAVE framework.
The discussion forms a reasoned argument with evidence of critical evaluation and synthesis.
weaknesses: Some analytic points are surface-level, and occasional internal inconsistencies (e.g., control variables) weaken depth.
Structural slips (e.g., redundancy in the conclusion, the â0 studiesâ misprint) interrupt the flow of argument.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three criticalâthinking strands (research quality, analysis depth and evaluation) sit solidly in the 7â9 band. The studentâs work approaches the top of that band through balanced source use and reasoned conclusions, but uneven depth in parts prevents placement in the 10â12 range, warranting a score of 9.

strengths: Structure adheres fully to academic conventions: title page, contents, headings and pagination are all present and logical.
Layout and formatting are consistently applied, with correct citation style and clear labeling.
weaknesses: A few minor typographical artefacts and spacing issues remain.
Some tables and headers could be better demarcated to enhance readability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets all required conventions without omission. Logical flow, clear navigation and consistent formatting justify the top mark of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
Terminology such as âcross-sectional correlational,â âlongitudinal,â âmultiple regression,â âconstruct validity,â âtrait aggression,â âdemand characteristics,â and âpublication biasâ is used accurately and in appropriate contexts.  Abbreviations (ESRB, GRAVE, STAXI, SDQ) are spelled out and applied correctly.

Concepts are consistently woven into evaluation, e.g., distinguishing correlation from causation, discussing ecological validity, and identifying confounding variables like competitiveness or socio-economic factors.  Occasional slips (e.g., confusing r and rÂ² in one sentence) are minor and do not impede clarity.

The frequent, precise deployment of discipline-specific language meets the descriptor for accurate and consistent usage; a slight deduction for the few notational inaccuracies yields 5.

Formatting is consistently handled: word count stated (4000), title shown, pagination visible, and the prose stays within the limit.  Tables are labelled, headings styled hierarchically, citations follow a single recognised system, and Works Cited is alphabetised.

Minor typographical issues (occasional page-break artefacts, errant spaces) do not breach conventions.  Therefore the layout meets all listed requirements, warranting full marks (4) in the highest band.

The essay demonstrates wide, accurate command of psychological content: definitions of aggression, discussion of ESRB ratings, explanation of experimental paradigms (Hot-Sauce, STAXI), and statistical concepts (effect size, regression, publication bias).  Summaries of each study capture core findings and methodological details correctly (sample size, correlations, p-values, effect sizes).

Sources are integrated coherently â e.g., contrasting Anderson et al.âs correlation (r = 0.35) with Ferguson & Kilburnâs rÂ² =.08, and synthesising how methodological quality moderates outcomes.  Citations are consistently given in-text and in Works Cited, showing systematic application.

Minor inaccuracies (claim of reviewing â0 studiesâ, occasional mis-labelling of rÂ² versus r) prevent a flawless profile, but the knowledge remains âexcellentâ¦ with consistently relevant source application,â supporting 5 in the 5â6 band.

The investigation draws on a broad, balanced collection of peer-reviewed studies, meta-analyses and empirical papers all directly relevant to the RQ.  Each sourceâs relevance is justified (e.g., Adachi & Willoughby for competitiveness nuance, Przybylski & Weinstein for null findings).  Currency and diversity (2007-2019, different methodologies and cultures) further strengthen quality.

While overall selection is strong, reliance on mostly Western samples is acknowledged as a limitation, and there is a stray statistical headline reference (Bushman & Huesmann smoking analogy) that is less empirically tight.  These small issues keep the strand within âgoodâ¦ mostly relevantâ rather than âexcellentâ¦ consistently.â

Hence an 8, toward the upper end of the 7â9 band, is justified.

The research question â âTo what extent do violent video games influence aggressive behaviour in adolescents?â â is sharply formulated, specific in population (adolescents) and in variable (violent video games â aggression).  It stays foregrounded as each study is summarised and evaluated; the conclusion explicitly answers it.

Focus is occasionally diluted by minor inconsistencies (the plan says â0 studiesâ then lists several; the competitiveness variable slightly broadens the focus).  Nonetheless the discussion consistently circles back to whether and how violent content affects aggression, so the partial deviations do not undermine overall alignment.

Given the clear statement and sustained linkage, but with a few lapses in precision, the strand aligns with the top band yet merits the lower half (5).

The report follows academic convention: title page with key data, abstract-style introduction, contents page, sectional headings, numbered pages and a comprehensive reference list.  The progression of ideas is logical and mirrors the initial overview, facilitating reader navigation.

All essential EE/IA structural elements are present; no required component is missing.  Hence the work matches âclearly appropriate structure meeting all conventions,â justifying the top mark of 4 in the 3â4 band.

The discussion is logically organised: supportive evidence, refuting evidence, then implications.  The conclusion acknowledges nuance and multifactorial causation, directly answering the RQ and recommending future research directions (cross-cultural, longitudinal).  Integration of methodological critique into the final stance shows an emergent critical evaluation.

Yet the argument occasionally relies on assertions (âviolent games likely one of many factorsâ) without fully synthesising quantitative weight of evidence, and some structural slips (misprint â0 studiesâ) detract from polish.  Still, the essay moves beyond basic summary to reasoned judgement, aligning with âreasoned argument with partially critical evaluation.â

Placement at 7 within the 7â9 band accurately reflects this quality.

Analysis proceeds study-by-study using GRAVE, identifying strengths (statistical controls, random assignment) and weaknesses (self-report, ecological validity).  The student triangulates findings (e.g., competitiveness as moderating variable) and contrasts differing meta-analytic outcomes, demonstrating clear reasoning.

However, depth is uneven: some evaluations are surface-level (âethical approval obtainedâ without further commentary) and the statistical critique occasionally confuses r and rÂ².  Links between individual evaluations and the overarching argument are present but not always fully developed (e.g., competitiveness discussion not integrated into final synthesis).

Thus the work meets âclear analysis with mostly supported conclusions,â meriting the lower end of the 7â9 band â score 7.

annotations.comment: The introduction effectively situates the topic with a compelling analogy to public health and recent events, demonstrating strong subject understanding and relevance.
The name âHeusmannâ is misspelled; the correct author name is âHuesmann,â so verify citation accuracy to maintain source credibility.
Present the correlation coefficient in LaTeX, e.g., $$r = 0.30$$, to maintain consistency with statistical reporting standards.
The survey methodology is clearly outlined; however, discuss potential social desirability bias in adolescent self-reports to deepen critical evaluation.
The title is clearly stated at the start, providing focus and context for the investigation, aligning with the requirement for a descriptive title.
The declared word count of 4000 meets the formatting requirement, ensuring adherence to the maximum word limit.
The research question is explicitly stated and focuses on the extent of violent video gamesâ influence on adolescent aggression, demonstrating a clear and appropriate focus.
The table of contents accurately mirrors the essay structure, facilitating easy navigation and coherence between sections.
Definition of âaggressive behaviourâ and WHO age range is precise and well-sourced, providing clear operational terms for the investigation.
The statement indicating âreview 0 studiesâ contradicts the subsequent content; correct the overview to match the actual number of studies for consistency.
Clear signposting in section headers enhances the logical flow and reinforces methodological clarity throughout the essay.
When presenting correlations, use LaTeX formatting such as $$r = 0.35$$ to improve academic precision and readability.
The text states the study did not control for gender, yet earlier it mentions statistical controls; ensure consistency when discussing validity to avoid confusion.
The description of experimental conditions and measurement tools for Adachi and Willoughbyâs study is thorough, demonstrating strong methodological understanding.
The table clearly summarises competitiveness and aggression outcomes, but it lacks a numbered title and formatted caption to conform with academic conventions.
The narrative interpretation of the table is detailed; consider dividing this paragraph into smaller segments to enhance reader engagement.
Ethical considerations for the Hot Sauce Paradigm are mentioned; strengthen this by addressing participant debriefing and psychological support protocols.
The multiple regression method is well-explained; linking its contribution directly back to the RQ would strengthen focus.
The notation âr<sup>2</sup> = .08â is misformatted; use $$r^2 = 0.08$$ for clarity and correctness in effect size reporting.
The ecological validity critique is insightful; consider providing a concrete example contrasting lab tasks with real-world gaming behaviours.
The commentary on generalisability acknowledges sample limitations; integrating a suggestion for cross-cultural replication would enhance the evaluative depth.
The use of discriminant function analysis is appropriate; include reported $$p$$-values or classification accuracy metrics to support the predictive claims.
The âImplications and Conclusionâ header could be formatted to start on a new page or section for clearer demarcation.
The final paragraph offers a valuable summary; adding a brief restatement of methodological limitations would reinforce critical evaluation.
The conclusion effectively answers the research question but repeats earlier points; synthesise key findings more concisely to avoid redundancy.
The Works Cited list is comprehensive and alphabetised, meeting formatting conventions for references.
Some citations lack hanging indent formatting; ensure each entry follows the chosen style guide consistently.
Including DOI links enhances source traceability; format each DOI uniformly (e.g., https://doi.org/â¦) to maintain consistency.
